back_translation: So these three men stopped answering Job, because he was righteous in his own eyes.
This kindled the anger of Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite, of the family of Ram. He was angry at Job for claiming himself righteous rather than acknowledging God as righteous,
and he was also angry at Job's three friends, because they could not refute Job and yet had condemned him.
Elihu had waited to speak to Job because the others were older than he.
But when Elihu saw that the three men had no more answer, his anger was kindled.
So Elihu son of Barachel the Buzite said: 'I am young in years, while you are old; that is why I held back and was afraid to tell you what I know.
I thought, 'Those who are older should speak, and those of many years should teach wisdom.'
But there is a spirit in man, the breath of the Almighty, that gives him understanding.
It is not only the aged who are wise, nor only the old who understand justice.
Therefore I say, 'Listen to me; I too will tell what I know.'
Behold, I waited while you spoke; I listened to your reasoning as you searched for words.
I paid you full attention, but behold, no one proved Job wrong; not one of you refuted his words.
So do not say, 'We have found wisdom; let God refute him, not man.'
But Job has not aimed his words at me, and I will not answer him with your arguments.
Job's friends are dismayed and can say no more; words have escaped them.
Should I wait further, now that they are silent, now that they stand still and answer no more?
I too will answer; yes, I too will tell what I know.
For I am full of words, and the spirit within me compels me to speak.
Behold, my belly is like wine with no vent, ready to burst like new wineskins.
I must speak so that I find relief; I must open my lips and reply.
I will show partiality to no one, and I will not flatter anyone.
For I do not know how to flatter; otherwise my Maker would remove me in an instant.
